cards.title: Common Drug Side Effects — BNYS Relevance
cards._legacyHtml:
Why This Matters for BNYS
Many patients present to naturopathic clinics with symptoms that are actually drug side effects. Recognising these prevents misdiagnosis and inappropriate treatment. Always review the medication list before attributing symptoms to disease.
Drug ClassCommon ExamplesKey Side EffectsNaturopathic Management
StatinsAtorvastatin, rosuvastatinMyalgia/myopathy (5–10%), fatigue, CoQ10 depletion, liver enzyme ↑CoQ10 100–200mg/day, monitor CK, magnesium for muscle cramps
ACE inhibitorsEnalapril, ramipril, lisinoprilDry cough (10–15%), hyperkalaemia, angioedema (rare but dangerous)Explain cough is drug effect; angioedema = emergency
MetforminGlucophageGI upset, nausea, diarrhoea (30%), B12 deficiency (long-term)Take with food, B12 monitoring/supplementation, probiotics for GI
PPIsOmeprazole, pantoprazoleMg deficiency, B12 deficiency, increased fracture risk, C.diff risk, SIBOMagnesium, B12, DGL liquorice, consider deprescribing with diet
SSRIsSertraline, fluoxetine, escitalopramNausea (initial), sexual dysfunction, weight gain, insomnia/somnolence, emotional bluntingZinc, Mg, omega-3 as adjuncts; never stop abruptly — taper only
NSAIDsIbuprofen, diclofenac, naproxenGI ulceration, renal impairment, fluid retention, CVD risk (COX-2)Always with food; natural alternatives: turmeric, willow bark, omega-3
CorticosteroidsPrednisolone, dexamethasoneWeight gain, hyperglycaemia, osteoporosis, adrenal suppression, Cushing's featuresCalcium+D3, vitamin K2, blood glucose monitoring; never stop abruptly
Thyroid (levothyroxine)ThyroxinePalpitations, insomnia, tremor if over-replaced; hair loss initiallyTake 30–60 min before food; calcium/iron supplements 4 hrs apart; monitor TSH
Antihypertensives (beta-blockers)Metoprolol, atenololFatigue, cold extremities, exercise intolerance, depression, erectile dysfunctionCoQ10, magnesium; do not stop abruptly (rebound hypertension)
AntibioticsAmoxicillin, ciprofloxacinGI upset, dysbiosis, C.diff (clindamycin/ciprofloxacin), photosensitivity (quinolones)Probiotics (2 hrs away from dose), during and 4 weeks after course

cards.title: Drug-Nutrient Depletions — Key Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
DrugNutrient DepletedSymptoms of DepletionReplacement Dose
StatinsCoQ10Fatigue, muscle weakness, myalgiaCoQ10 100–200mg/day with fat
MetforminB12, folateNeuropathy, macrocytic anaemia, cognitive declineMethylcobalamin 1000mcg/day, methylfolate 400mcg
PPIsMg, B12, Ca, Zn, FeMuscle cramps, fatigue, fractures, anaemiaMg glycinate 300mg, B12 1000mcg, Ca+D3
Oral contraceptivesB6, B12, folate, Zn, Mg, CDepression, PMS, fatigue, poor wound healingB-complex + Zn 15mg + Mg 300mg + Vit C 500mg
ACE inhibitors/diureticsZinc, Mg, potassiumTaste changes, muscle cramps, weaknessZn 15mg, Mg 300mg, potassium-rich foods
CorticosteroidsCa, Mg, K, B6, Zn, Vit C, DOsteoporosis, immune suppression, wound healingCa 1000mg+D3 2000IU+K2 100mcg, Zn, Mg
Antibiotics (prolonged)Vitamins B, K, beneficial gut floraDysbiosis, fatigue, immune suppressionB-complex, probiotics, vit K2
Beta-blockersCoQ10, melatoninFatigue, exercise intolerance, sleep disturbanceCoQ10 100mg, melatonin 1–3mg at night
🟡 Pearl — Deprescribing Opportunity
PPIs are the most overprescribed medication in primary care. 70% of PPI prescriptions have no clear indication after review. Naturopathic approach to PPI deprescribing: (1) Identify true indication (GERD vs functional). (2) Optimise diet (no late meals, no trigger foods, elevate HOB). (3) DGL liquorice 400mg before meals. (4) Reduce PPI by 50% every 2 weeks (rebound acid secretion = most common reason for failure). (5) H2 blocker bridge during taper. Success rate with naturopathic support: 65–70%.

cards.title: Herb-Drug Interactions — Quick Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
Critical Interactions ★
St John's Wort interacts with 70+ drugs via CYP450 induction. Always take a full supplement history from every patient.
HerbDrug ClassEffectSeverity
St John's WortSSRIs, triptansSerotonin syndrome🔴 Severe
St John's WortWarfarin, OCP, antiretrovirals, digoxin, cyclosporinDrug levels ↓ → treatment failure🔴 Severe — CYP3A4/P-gp induction
Ginkgo bilobaWarfarin, aspirin, NSAIDs, clopidogrelBleeding risk ↑🔴 Severe
Licorice rootAntihypertensives, digoxin, diureticsHTN ↑, hypokalaemia, digoxin toxicity🟡 Moderate
ValerianBenzodiazepines, anaesthetics, alcoholCNS depression ↑🟡 Moderate
EchinaceaImmunosuppressants (cyclosporin, tacrolimus)Immunosuppression ↓🟡 Moderate
GinsengWarfarin, insulin, MAOIsINR ↓, hypoglycaemia, serotonin syndrome🟡 Moderate
Kava kavaHepatotoxic drugs, alcohol, CNS depressantsHepatotoxicity ↑, CNS depression ↑🔴 Severe
Garlic (high dose)Warfarin, aspirinBleeding risk ↑🟡 Moderate
Turmeric (high dose)Warfarin, NSAIDsMild bleeding risk🟢 Mild
🟡 Stop Before Surgery
Stop ALL herbal supplements 2 weeks before elective surgery. Key ones: Garlic, Ginkgo, Ginseng, Ginger, St John's Wort, Valerian, Echinacea, Feverfew, Kava. They affect bleeding, anaesthesia depth, or wound healing. Inform the anaesthetist of all supplements at pre-assessment.

cards.title: Nutrient Synergy Matrix — Combinations That Amplify Each Other
cards._legacyHtml:
Synergy Principle
Nutrients do not work in isolation — many have profound synergistic relationships where combining them produces effects far greater than either alone. Understanding synergy lets you design more effective protocols with lower doses and reduced cost. Conversely, certain combinations create antagonism and must be avoided.
Nutrient ANutrient BSynergy EffectMechanismClinical Use
Vitamin D3Vitamin K2 (MK-7)✅ Essential pairD3 ↑ calcium absorption; K2 directs calcium into bone (osteocalcin) and away from arteries (MGP). D3 without K2 = calcium deposits in arteries.All Ca+D3 protocols. Dose: D3 1000–5000 IU + K2 100–200mcg MK-7
IronVitamin C✅ Absorption ↑ 3×Vit C reduces Fe³⁺ → Fe²⁺ (absorbable form). Chelates iron in gut lumen maintaining solubility at higher pH.Iron deficiency anaemia. Iron bisglycinate 25mg + Vit C 250mg together. Separate from tea/coffee/calcium by 2hrs.
MagnesiumVitamin D3✅ Activation cofactorMg is required for every enzymatic step of Vit D activation (25-hydroxylation in liver; 1α-hydroxylation in kidney). Mg deficiency renders Vit D supplementation ineffective regardless of dose.Always supplement Mg when giving therapeutic Vit D. Mg glycinate 300–400mg/day.
CurcuminPiperine (BioPerine)✅ Bioavailability ↑ 2000%Piperine inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein in gut wall — dramatically slows curcumin metabolism. Raises absorption from ~1% to ~20%.All curcumin protocols. Always use combined product or add 5–10mg piperine. Caution: piperine potentiates some drugs — check interactions.
ZincVitamin A✅ Mutual activationZinc required for retinol-binding protein synthesis (transports Vit A). Without zinc, Vit A remains sequestered in liver stores.Immune support, skin, wound healing. Zn 15–30mg + Vit A 5000IU. Critical in malnourished patients.
CoQ10Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)✅ Cardiovascular synergyCoQ10 restores mitochondrial ATP efficiency; omega-3 reduces membrane viscosity and inflammatory load. Additive cardioprotection via independent pathways.CVD, heart failure, statin myopathy. CoQ10 ubiquinol 200mg + omega-3 3g/day.
Vitamin CVitamin E✅ Antioxidant recyclingVit E quenches lipid ROS but becomes a radical itself. Vit C (ascorbate) regenerates Vit E from its radical form — extending antioxidant capacity without increasing dose.Oxidative stress, cardiovascular prevention, photoprotection. Vit C 1g + Vit E 400IU mixed tocopherols.
NACGlycine✅ Glutathione synthesisGlutathione = Glutamate + Cysteine + Glycine. NAC provides cysteine (rate-limiting). Glycine is often deficient in ageing. GlyNAC restores glutathione better than either alone.Ageing, mitochondrial dysfunction, liver disease. GlyNAC: glycine 1.1g + NAC 0.8g per 10kg body weight.
ProbioticsPrebiotics (FOS/inulin)✅ Synbiotic effectPrebiotics selectively feed supplemented probiotic strains — increasing colonisation, survival, and SCFA/butyrate production. Probiotics alone are transient; prebiotics extend their benefit.Gut dysbiosis, IBS, immune support. Lactobacillus + inulin 5g/day. Separate from antibiotics by 2hrs.
BerberineAlpha-lipoic acid✅ Insulin sensitisation ×2Berberine activates AMPK via mitochondrial complex I inhibition; ALA activates AMPK via upstream SIRT1 and directly stimulates GLUT4 translocation. Additive glucose lowering from independent mechanisms.T2DM, insulin resistance, PCOS, metabolic syndrome. Berberine 500mg TID + ALA 600mg/day.
5-HTPVitamin B6 (P5P)✅ Serotonin synthesis5-HTP → serotonin requires aromatic amino acid decarboxylase — a B6-dependent enzyme. Without P5P, 5-HTP is decarboxylated peripherally causing nausea rather than centrally in the brain.Depression, insomnia, anxiety. 5-HTP 100–300mg + P5P 25–50mg. Take P5P 30 min before 5-HTP.
SeleniumIodine✅ Thyroid protection pairIodine is required for T4 synthesis. Selenium (as GPx and thioredoxin reductase) protects the thyroid from H₂O₂ generated during iodine organification. Iodine without selenium → thyroid oxidative damage → autoimmunity.Thyroid support, Hashimoto's. Always give Se 100–200mcg when supplementing iodine. Selenium alone ↓ TPO antibodies 50% (Cochrane).

cards.title: Nutrient Antagonism Matrix — Combinations to AVOID
cards._legacyHtml:
⚠️ Antagonistic Pairs — These Block Each Other or Cause Harm
Giving these together wastes money and can cause harm. Space them by 2–4 hours minimum, or choose non-competing forms.
Nutrient AAntagonist BProblemSolution
IronCalcium🔴 Compete for DMT-1 transporter in duodenum → iron absorption ↓ 60%Separate by ≥2 hours. Iron with Vit C in AM on empty stomach; calcium with meals or PM.
IronZinc (high dose)🔴 Compete for same intestinal transporter. Zinc:iron ratio >2:1 inhibits iron absorption.Separate by ≥2hrs. At therapeutic iron doses, avoid simultaneous zinc >15mg.
Zinc (high dose)Copper🔴 Zinc induces metallothionein in gut cells → binds copper → blocks absorption. Chronic high zinc → copper deficiency anaemia and neuropathy.For every 15mg zinc, add 1mg copper. Do not exceed 40mg/day without monitoring. Signs: anaemia unresponsive to iron, peripheral neuropathy.
Folate (high dose)B12 deficiency (masked)🔴 High-dose folate corrects macrocytic anaemia while neurological B12 damage continues silently — degeneration becomes irreversible.ALWAYS confirm and correct B12 before giving folate. Never give folate >1mg without checking B12 status. Use methylcobalamin.
Calcium (high dose)Levothyroxine🔴 Calcium chelates levothyroxine in GI tract → absorption ↓ 30–40% → hypothyroidism inadequately controlled.Separate levothyroxine from calcium, iron, and magnesium by minimum 4 hours. Take thyroxine first thing on empty stomach.
5-HTP / TryptophanSSRIs / MAOIs🔴 Risk of serotonin syndrome — agitation, hyperthermia, clonus, autonomic instability. Potentially fatal.Never combine 5-HTP with SSRIs/SNRIs without medical supervision. MAOI + 5-HTP = absolute contraindication.
Green tea / Quercetin / CoffeeIron supplements🟡 Polyphenols chelate iron in gut lumen → absorption reduced 50–90%Do not take iron with tea, coffee, or polyphenol supplements. Separate by 2 hours minimum.
Vit A (mega-dose)Vitamin D receptor🟡 At pharmacological doses (>25,000IU), Vit A antagonises Vit D receptor activity — reduces D3 efficacy.Keep Vit A within physiological range (5000–10,000 IU). Do not use mega-dose Vit A when optimising Vit D therapy.

cards.title: Drug-Nutrient Synergy — Using Drugs Better With Nutrition
cards._legacyHtml:
Integrative Principle
Many conventional drugs work better, are better tolerated, and can sometimes be dose-reduced when combined with targeted nutritional support. This is evidence-based integrative medicine — not replacing drugs, but enhancing their effectiveness and reducing side effects.
DrugNutritional SynergistEffectEvidence
StatinsCoQ10 ubiquinol 200mg/dayRestores CoQ10 depleted by statin (shared mevalonate pathway). Reduces statin myalgia 54%. Improves exercise tolerance and energy.★★★ Multiple RCTs
MetforminBerberine 500mg TID + ALA 600mg + Mg 400mgAdditive glucose lowering via complementary AMPK pathways. Berberine allows lower metformin doses. Addresses B12/folate depletion.★★★ RCT: comparable HbA1c reduction to metformin 1500mg
SSRIsEPA-predominant omega-3 2–4g/day + methylfolate 15mgOmega-3 augments antidepressant response — 50% better response than SSRI alone. Methylfolate (MTHFR support) further augments response in poor methylators.★★★ Meta-analysis (n=1,233)
PPIs (during taper)DGL liquorice 400mg + zinc carnosine 75mg + melatonin 6mgDGL stimulates mucus; zinc carnosine repairs mucosal barrier; melatonin reduces gastric acid and heals LES. Bridge during PPI deprescribing (50% dose reduction every 2 weeks).★★ Individual RCTs
ACE inhibitors / ARBsCoQ10 200mg + Mg glycinate 400mg + beet juice 250mlAdditive 5–17mmHg systolic reduction via independent vasodilation pathways. Addresses Mg depletion from co-prescribed diuretics. May allow dose reduction over 6 months.★★★ CoQ10 + Mg meta-analyses
Chemotherapy (supportive)Glutamine 10–30g/day + Vit D + omega-3Glutamine reduces CIPN severity and mucositis; protects gut barrier during fluorouracil. Omega-3 preserves muscle mass (cancer cachexia). Only with oncologist approval.★★★ Multiple RCTs for glutamine in mucositis/CIPN
WarfarinVit K2 MK-7 45–90mcg consistent dailyStable low-dose K2 improves INR stability — inconsistent dietary Vit K is the main cause of INR fluctuation. Counter-intuitive but evidence-based.★★ RCT: reduces INR variability 30%. Only under anticoagulation clinic supervision.
🟡 Communication Rule — Never Reduce Drugs Unilaterally
Any nutritional synergy that may allow drug dose reduction MUST be coordinated with the prescribing physician. Your role: document the evidence, discuss with the patient, communicate with the prescriber in writing. A brief clinical note with the evidence basis builds referral relationships and protects both patient and practitioner legally. Never advise dose reduction without prescriber knowledge.

cards.title: Comprehensive Depletion Reference — All Major Drug Classes
cards._legacyHtml:
Drug / ClassDepleted NutrientsKey ManifestationsReplacement ProtocolPriority
StatinsCoQ10 ★★★, Vit D, seleniumMyalgia, fatigue, exercise intolerance, cognitive fogCoQ10 ubiquinol 200mg, Vit D 2000IU, Se 100mcg🔴 Start with statin
MetforminB12 ★★★, folate, thiaminePeripheral neuropathy, macrocytic anaemia, ↑ homocysteineMethylcobalamin 1000mcg/day, methylfolate 400mcg, B1 100mg. Check B12 annually.🔴 Monitor annually
PPIsMg ★★★, B12 ★★★, Ca, Zn, Fe, Vit CHypomagnesaemia (cramps, arrhythmia), B12 deficiency, fracturesMg glycinate 300mg, sublingual B12 1000mcg, Ca+D3, Zn 15mg🔴 Especially if >3 months
Oral ContraceptivesB6 ★★★, B12, folate, Zn ★★, Mg, Vit C, seleniumDepression, PMS, poor libido, fatigue, ↑ homocysteineB-complex with P5P 25mg + methylfolate 400mcg + methylcobalamin + Zn 15mg + Mg 300mg🔴 Mental health critical
CorticosteroidsCa ★★★, Vit D ★★★, Mg, K, B6, Zn, Vit C, DHEAOsteoporosis, muscle wasting, hyperglycaemia, immune suppressionCa 1000mg + D3 3000IU + K2 100mcg (mandatory >3 months). Mg 400mg, Vit C 1g, Zn 15mg.🔴 Bone protection critical
Thiazide diureticsMg ★★★, K ★★★, Zn, B1, CoQ10Muscle cramps, weakness, arrhythmia, fatigue, worsened diabetesMg glycinate 400mg, K-rich diet (monitor serum K), Zn 15mg, B1 100mg🔴 Arrhythmia risk
Loop diureticsMg ★★★, K ★★★, Ca, Zn, B1, B6, Vit CAs thiazides but more severe. High risk of hypomagnesaemia-induced arrhythmia.Mg glycinate 400–600mg, K (physician-prescribed), Ca+D3, B-complex, Vit C 500mg🔴 Cardiac monitoring
Beta-blockersCoQ10 ★★, melatonin, Vit DFatigue, exercise intolerance, insomniaCoQ10 ubiquinol 150mg, melatonin 0.5–1mg at bedtime, Vit D 2000IU🟡 Moderate
SSRIs/SNRIsZn ★★, Mg, folate, Vit D, melatoninWorsened depression, sleep disruption, sexual dysfunction. Elderly: hyponatraemia (SIADH).Zn 15mg, Mg glycinate 300mg, methylfolate 400–800mcg, Vit D 2000IU. Monitor Na in elderly.🟡 Moderate
AnticonvulsantsFolate ★★★, B12, Vit D ★★, Ca, biotin, Vit K, L-carnitine (valproate)Megaloblastic anaemia, osteoporosis, NTD risk, carnitine deficiencyMethylfolate 5mg/day (women of childbearing age), Vit D 3000IU + Ca 1000mg, biotin 5mg, L-carnitine 1g if on valproate🔴 Folate critical in women
Antibiotics (prolonged)Vit K ★★★, B vitamins, gut floraBleeding tendency (K), dysbiosis, immune suppression, C.diff riskProbiotics (LGG + Saccharomyces boulardii) 2hrs from dose + 4 weeks post-course. Vit K2 45mcg if prolonged.🟡 Moderate

cards.title: Dangerous Drug Combinations — Must Know
cards._legacyHtml:
⚠️ These Combinations Can Kill — Screen Before Adding Any Natural Therapy
BNYS practitioners must recognise dangerous drug combinations patients may already be on before adding any natural intervention.
CombinationRiskMechanismAction
MAOI + any serotonergic drug (SSRIs, TCAs, tramadol, pethidine, triptans, St John's Wort, 5-HTP)🔴 FATAL — Serotonin SyndromeMAO inhibition + serotonin agonism → massive serotonin accumulation → hyperthermia, clonus, seizures, cardiac arrhythmia, death14-day washout after MAOI before any serotonergic drug. Absolute contraindication.
Warfarin + St John's Wort / Ginkgo / high-dose fish oil / Vit E🔴 Severe — Haemorrhage or ThrombosisSJW (CYP3A4 induction) → ↓ warfarin → thrombosis. Antiplatelet herbs → ↑ bleeding risk. Unpredictable INR.Check INR within 1 week of ANY herbal addition. Avoid SJW completely. Stop Ginkgo/Garlic 2 weeks before procedures.
ACE inhibitors + K-sparing diuretics + K supplements🔴 Severe — Hyperkalaemia → Cardiac arrestTriple K-retention pathway → serum K >6.5 → VF/VT. Classic in heart failure patients on ACEi + spironolactone.Never give K supplements without checking serum K in patients on ACEi + spironolactone.
Methotrexate + NSAIDs🔴 Severe — MTX toxicityNSAIDs displace MTX from plasma proteins + reduce renal clearance → MTX accumulation → bone marrow suppression, deathNever give NSAIDs (including ibuprofen) to patients on methotrexate without haematology review.
Digoxin + hypokalaemia-inducing drugs (thiazides, loop diuretics, laxatives)🔴 Severe — Digoxin toxicityLow K → digoxin receptor hypersensitivity → toxicity at therapeutic levels → bradycardia, heart block, VTMonitor K obsessively in digoxin patients. Target K >4.0 mmol/L. Mg supplementation supports K retention.
Lithium + NSAIDs / ACE inhibitors / thiazides🔴 Severe — Lithium toxicityNSAIDs reduce prostaglandin-mediated renal blood flow → ↓ lithium clearance. ACEi/thiazides → compensatory Li reabsorption → toxicity.Use paracetamol only for pain in lithium patients. Never add ACEi/thiazide without lithium level monitoring.
Sildenafil + Nitrates (GTN, isosorbide)🔴 FATAL — Severe hypotensionBoth converge on cGMP → profound vasodilation → cardiac arrest. Irreversible once taken together.ABSOLUTE contraindication. Ask every ED patient about nitrate use before recommending any ED herbs or supplements.

cards.title: CYP450 Interactions — Practical Clinical Guide
cards._legacyHtml:
CYP450 System — Why It Matters
The cytochrome P450 enzyme system metabolises 70–80% of all drugs. Herbs, foods, and supplements that inhibit (raise drug levels) or induce (lower drug levels) CYP enzymes create clinically significant interactions.
EnzymeKey Drugs MetabolisedInhibitors (↑ drug levels)Inducers (↓ drug levels)
CYP3A4 (50% of drugs)Statins (simvastatin, atorvastatin), cyclosporin, tacrolimus, HIV meds, Ca channel blockers, midazolam, sildenafilGrapefruit juice ★★★ (72hr irreversible), ketoconazole, clarithromycin, ritonavir, pomegranate juice, piperine (mild)St John's Wort ★★★, rifampicin, carbamazepine, phenytoin → drug levels ↓ → treatment failure
CYP2D6Codeine (activation), beta-blockers, SSRIs, TCAs, tamoxifen (activation), tramadolFluoxetine, paroxetine (self-inhibiting), bupropion, curcumin (mild)Genetic polymorphism — poor metabolisers cannot activate codeine (no analgesia) or tamoxifen (cancer treatment failure)
CYP2C9Warfarin (S-isomer), NSAIDs, phenytoin, glipizide, losartan activationFluconazole ★★★ (warfarin INR doubles — life-threatening), amiodarone, miconazole (topical also!), cranberry juice (mild)Rifampicin, carbamazepine, St John's Wort → warfarin ↓ → thrombosis
CYP1A2Caffeine, theophylline, clozapine, haloperidol, olanzapineFluvoxamine ★★★ (raises clozapine/olanzapine → sedation, seizure), ciprofloxacin, cimetidineSmoking ★★★ — smokers need higher antipsychotic doses; on quitting, levels rise → toxicity
🟡 Grapefruit Warning — Practical List
Grapefruit furanocoumarins irreversibly inhibit intestinal CYP3A4 for 72 hours. One glass can raise simvastatin levels 3–16× → rhabdomyolysis. Avoid with: simvastatin ★★★, lovastatin, cyclosporin, tacrolimus, felodipine, midazolam, buspirone, sildenafil. Atorvastatin: low risk (hepatic metabolism). Rosuvastatin: safe (not CYP3A4 substrate). Always ask statin and immunosuppressant patients about grapefruit consumption.

cards.title: Pharmaceutical-Naturopathic Combination Protocols
cards._legacyHtml:
Evidence-Based Drug + Natural Combinations
Validated combinations where evidence supports using both — producing better outcomes than either alone, with fewer side effects. Always coordinate with prescribing physician.
ConditionDrugNaturopathic AdditionBenefitEvidence
T2DMMetformin 1000mg BDBerberine 500mg TID + ALA 600mg + chromium 200mcg + Mg 400mgAdditive HbA1c reduction; addresses B12/folate depletion; improves insulin sensitivity via additional pathways★★★
HypertensionACE inhibitor / ARBCoQ10 200mg + Mg glycinate 400mg + beet juice 250ml/dayAdditive 5–17mmHg systolic reduction. May allow dose reduction over 6 months.★★★
DepressionSSRI (sertraline, escitalopram)EPA omega-3 2g/day + methylfolate 15mg + Zn 15mg + Vit D 3000IU50% better response than SSRI alone. Addresses all nutrient depletions.★★★
OsteoporosisBisphosphonate (alendronate)Ca 1000mg + D3 4000IU + K2 MK-7 180mcg + Mg 400mgDrug prevents resorption; naturopathic protocol drives formation. Combined superior for BMD.★★★
HypothyroidismLevothyroxineSelenium 200mcg + Zn 15mg + Mg 300mg (timed apart from thyroxine)Se ↓ TPO antibodies 50%, improves T4→T3. Zn activates T3 receptors. Separate from thyroxine by 4hrs.★★★ Se Cochrane; ★★ Zn
IBD — UC maintenanceMesalamine (5-ASA)Curcumin 3g/day + probiotics (VSL#3) + omega-3 3gCurcumin RCT equivalent to mesalamine for maintenance; combined reduces relapse to <5%/year.★★★
